task: Design a RAG system for a customer support chatbot with 500 help articles. The articles are in Markdown, updated weekly, and the chatbot needs to answer questions with source citations in under 3 seconds.
expect: Recommends a chunking strategy appropriate for structured Markdown content
Selects an embedding model and vector database with justification
Designs the retrieval pipeline including hybrid search or re-ranking
Addresses context window management and source attribution
Includes an evaluation plan with faithfulness and relevance metrics
Considers the weekly update cycle in the ingestion pipeline design

task: A production prompt that extracts structured JSON from customer emails is generating inconsistent output. About 20% of responses fail JSON validation. The prompt works fine in testing with curated examples. Evaluate and optimise it.
expect: Investigates the gap between test and production inputs (real emails are messier)
Reviews the prompt structure for ambiguous instructions or missing edge cases
Recommends structured output enforcement (JSON mode, tool use, or schema validation)
Proposes a retry-with-fallback strategy for validation failures
Designs a prompt regression test suite using production failure examples
Suggests monitoring to catch future regressions early

task: An AI-powered product recommendation feature works well in testing but fails unpredictably in production — 5% of requests return nonsensical recommendations (wrong category, out-of-stock items, irrelevant to user history). The team cannot reproduce the issue locally. Diagnose and propose a fix.
expect: Identifies potential causes: stale embeddings, data pipeline lag, cache poisoning, model version drift
Proposes systematic diagnosis: log analysis, request sampling, A/B comparison of failing vs passing requests
Checks for data quality issues between test and production environments
Recommends output validation and guardrails before serving recommendations
Designs monitoring to detect and alert on quality degradation
Suggests a graceful fallback for when recommendations fail validation
